channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Harrowing escapes from Los Angeles wildfires were made by foot, by car or by the grace of strangers
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — Flames and pillars of smoke rose from both sides of the road and a woman yelled in panic as firefighters ushered a crowd of fleeing residents along. Aaron Samson positioned his 83-year-old father-in-law behind his blue walker, and they began shuffling down the sidewalk.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, BEATRICE DUPUY, HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, GENE JOHNSON and CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump, the 'America First' candidate, has a new preoccupation: Imperialism
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump ran on a return to his "America First” foreign policy platform. The U.S., he said, could no longer afford to be the world's policeman. On his watch, he pledged, there would be no new wars.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hollywood Hills fire breaks out as deadly wildfires burn out of control across Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A fast-moving fire broke out in the Hollywood Hills on Wednesday night, threatening one of Los Angeles' most iconic spots as firefighters battled to get under control three other major blazes that killed five people, put 130,000 people under evacuation orders and ravaged communiti
item.dc:creator: MANUEL VALDES, HALLIE GOLDEN and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about wildfires raining embers onto the Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — At least five people were killed and more than 1,000 structures were destroyed as fierce wildfires raged in the Los Angeles area, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader rallies support in Panama ahead of Maduro inauguration
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Days before Venezuela’s presidential inauguration, self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González was in Panama Wednesday rallying regional support for the opposition's claims that he won the July election against President Nicolás Maduro.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO and ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Silent on arrival: LA Mayor Karen Bass refuses to answer questions for her absence as wildfires ravage city
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass refused to answer a question after being pressed on her initial absence as wildfires raged across her jurisdiction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US will keep protecting more than 2,000 Rocky Mountain grizzly bears
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — The Biden administration said Wednesday it will continue protecting about 2,000 grizzly bears in the Rocky Mountains despite opposition from Republican-led states. Federal officials also said they would ease some restrictions as conflicts between bears and humans rise.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wildfires burning in the Los Angeles area visible from space
item.description: Satellite images from NOAA and CIRA show the Palisades and Eaton Fires burning in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KRON4

item.title: Monstrous wildfires blanket Southern California with smoky air, threatening the health of millions
item.description: Massive wildfires burning in the Los Angeles area have filled the air with a thick cloud of smoke and ash, prompting air quality advisories.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ex-police officer and Proud Boys member gets 14 months in prison for joining Capitol attack
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A former police officer who stormed the U.S. Capitol with fellow members of the far-right Proud Boys extremist group was sentenced on Wednesday to 14 months in prison for joining the Jan. 6, 2021, attack by a mob of Donald Trump supporters, court records show.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Oregon law cracks down on public drug use on buses and trains
item.description: "The changes passed in the last legislative session made it clear public drug use cannot be tolerated on transit systems." TriMet General Manager Sam Desue said.
item.dc:creator: Ariel Salk, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says he could've beaten Trump in November
item.description: “It’s presumptuous to say that, but I think yes,” the president said when reporter Susan Page asked if he could have won.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Biden casts doubt on his fitness to serve another four years days before term ends
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden, in a new interview days before he leaves office, cast doubt on his fitness to serve another four years even as he maintained that he could have won election to a second term.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New research shows 24% of freshwater animals threatened with extinction
item.description: Nearly a quarter of animals living in rivers, lakes and other freshwater sources are threatened with extinction, according to new research published Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON AP Science Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wildfires latest: Tens of thousands ordered to evacuate as fires tear through Los Angeles area
item.description: Fierce wildfires are raging in the Los Angeles area, fueled by powerful Santa Ana winds, sending residents fleeing from burning homes through flames, ferocious winds and towering clouds of smoke.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean court clears government, adoption agency of liability in adoptee's deportation from US
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court on Wednesday cleared the government and an adoption agency of all liability in a lawsuit filed by a 49-year-old Korean man whose traumatic adoption journey led to an abusive childhood in the United States and ultimately his deportation to South Korea in
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McDonald's rolls back diversity goals
item.description: Fast food giant McDonald’s has become the latest company to roll back diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) commitments following the Supreme Court’s 2023 decision ending affirmative action in college admissions.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KRON4

item.title: Officials use bulldozer to shove abandoned vehicles out of the way near Los Angeles fire
item.description: Drivers who abandoned their vehicles fleeing the growing fire in Los Angeles might find their cars a little worse for wear when they return.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, WGN 9

item.title: Lawyers collecting $148 million judgment from Rudy Giuliani say World Series rings must be given up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lawyers for two former Georgia election workers who are owed $148 million in damages after suing Rudy Giuliani for defamation said Tuesday that evidence proves their clients are entitled to three World Series rings that the former New York City mayor says he gave to his son.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Schools around the US are weighing responses to possible immigration raids
item.description: Schools around the country are reviewing what to do if immigration agents come knocking as President-elect Donald Trump prepares to take office after campaigning on a pledge to deport millions of people.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LGBTQ+ rights group reports progress at U.S. companies despite conservative backlash
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A national rights group said Tuesday that more U.S. companies are providing strong benefits and protections to LGBTQ+ employees despite an ongoing effort by conservative activists to get high-profile brands to stop participating in the organization's annual workplace report card.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Girl Scouts getting rid of 2 cookie offerings
item.description: The Girl Scouts of the USA have announced plans to discontinue two of their “beloved” cookie offerings after the current cookie season, which kicked off Tuesday. The organization revealed the news in a press release issued that morning.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: After 2 years of shortfalls, California governor proposes $322B budget with no deficit
item.description: TURLOCK, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom has proposed a $322 billion budget without a deficit, a welcome change after two years of significant budget shortfalls in the nation's most populous state.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden will honor tribal requests by designating 2 new national monuments in California
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is signing a proclamation to establish two new national monuments in California, in part to honor two tribes, a person familiar with the decision said Monday.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JAIMIE DING and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald's is the latest company to roll back diversity goals
item.description: Four years after launching a push for more diversity in its ranks, McDonald’s is ending some of its diversity practices, citing a U.S. Supreme Court decision that outlawed affirmative action in college admissions.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Uber and Lyft to offer driverless rideshare cars
item.description: Customers in Atlanta and Austin, Texas, will be able to hail one of these driverless rideshare cars via the Uber app.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, Fox 5

item.title: Rudy Giuliani is in contempt of court in $148 million defamation case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rudy Giuliani was found in contempt of court Monday for failing to properly respond to requests for information as he turned over assets to satisfy a $148 million defamation judgment granted to two Georgia election workers. Judge Lewis J.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hush money judge refuses to delay Trump's criminal sentencing
item.description: The New York judge who oversaw President-elect Trump’s criminal trial last spring declined his request to pause Friday’s sentencing, setting up a last-minute appellate battle as he seeks to stave off the proceeding.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: What is HMPV? CDC 'monitoring' reports of spiking cases in China
item.description: The CDC’s actions come in response to China's government-run media confirming that positivity rates of HMPV hare rising in children under the age of 14, despite respiratory illnesses being down overall since last year.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star allegedly choked, punched children's mother: police
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan, who was arrested last week in Myrtle Beach on a domestic violence charge, allegedly choked and punched the woman he lived with, according to a police report obtained by Nexstar's WBTW.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, ABC 4

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star choked, punched woman before Myrtle Beach arrest, report says
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan, who was arrested last week in Myrtle Beach on a domestic violence charge, allegedly choked and punched the woman he lived with, according to a police report obtained by WBTW.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Border agents in California find child allegedly abducted from Texas
item.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection said agents recovered a missing child last week and apprehended a driver wanted for their alleged abduction.
item.dc:creator: TMX, KTLA 5

item.title: Flight cancellations pile up as major winter storm hits East Coast
item.description: Flight cancellations and delays are stacking up as a major winter storm makes its way across the East Coast.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Winter weather can disrupt. Here's what to do if your flight is canceled
item.description: Winter weather often causes flight delays and cancellations. While airlines can’t control the weather, they are required in the U.S. to provide refunds to customers whose flights are canceled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: These winter driving 'tips' may be outdated and unnecessary, experts say
item.description: Driving in the winter is a different beast, especially when some of the most common advice for driving in the snow and cold isn't always right.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, ABC 4

item.title: Lawsuit alleges ex-Fox Sports host Skip Bayless harassed hairstylist, offered her $1.5 million for sex
item.description: The complaint claims Fox executives fostered a hostile work environment that allowed senior managers and on-air personalities including Bayless to abuse workers without fear of punishment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mother speaks out after father charged with killing his son on Christmas Eve
item.description: A Christmas Eve hangout turned deadly when a High Ridge father pulled a gun on his son and his son’s pregnant fiancé.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Pettey, KTLA 5
